text.title: ClickUp Workspace Architecture
text.summary: Hierarchy design, ClickApps configuration, custom status workflow, view setup, and custom field placement rules.

rule.statement: Keep hierarchy to 3–4 levels: Workspace → Space → Folder → List. Add Task/Subtask levels but never create a 6th level of nesting via checklists as a hierarchy substitute.
rule.rationale: Deeper hierarchies require more navigation clicks and degrade API query complexity; checklists cannot be queried as first-class items via API.

rule.statement: Use Spaces for department/product isolation with separate permissions and workflows; use Folders for project grouping, not timeline grouping.
rule.rationale: Permissions are set at Space level; timeline-based folders (Q1, Q2) become stale and require migration; project-based folders (Feature Dev, Bug Tracking) remain stable.

section.title: Custom Field Placement

rule.statement: Create custom fields at the highest applicable level (Workspace > Space > Folder > List) so they inherit downward.
rule.rationale: Fields created at List level must be recreated for every new list; Space-level fields are available to all lists in the Space automatically.

rule.statement: Document all created custom field IDs in a config file — field IDs are UUIDs, not human-readable names, and are required for API updates.
rule.rationale: Agents must match custom fields by ID, not name; names can be renamed by users, breaking agent queries silently.

section.reference: Custom status definitions JSON for development workflow

rule.statement: Enable at workspace level: Time Tracking (with estimates), Custom Fields, Priorities (Urgent/High/Normal/Low), Multiple Assignees. Enable Sprints at space level with 2-week duration and auto-close.
rule.rationale: Workspace-level ClickApps apply uniformly; space-level Sprints allow engineering spaces to use cycles while marketing spaces use continuous flow.

section.title: Views Setup

rule.statement: Create six standard views per space: List (detailed, group by status), Board (kanban, WIP limits), Gantt (timeline, show dependencies), Calendar (due dates), Workload (capacity, requires estimates), Dashboard (metrics).
rule.rationale: Each view serves a distinct PM function; missing the Workload view makes capacity planning invisible until tasks are already overdue.

section.reference: Software project folder and list hierarchy example
